operating_state: Net revenues declined 8% to $10.90 billion driven by significantly lower FICC intermediation revenues and investment banking fees reflecting reduced M&A activity and market-making volumes. Global Banking & Markets revenues fell with weak equity derivatives, interest rate products, and advisory revenues partially offset by improved mortgage results. Platform Solutions revenues increased driven by higher credit card balances and GreenSky inclusion, though pre-tax losses widened. Asset & Wealth Management revenues declined due to lower incentive fees from reduced harvesting activity and net losses in equity investments.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased 12% to $8.54 billion, primarily driven by $504 million goodwill impairment related to Consumer platforms and $485 million in commercial real estate impairments within consolidated investment entities. Efficiency ratio deteriorated to 78.4% from 64.5% despite 8% headcount reduction to 44,600 employees. Compensation remained stable with severance costs, while Platform Solutions expenses surged dominated by the goodwill impairment.
material_events: Goldman Sachs returned $1.61 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends during Q2 2023. The Board approved dividend increase from $2.50 to $2.75 per share beginning Q3 2023 and authorized a new $30 billion share repurchase program. The firm completed sale of substantially all Marcus consumer loan portfolio generating approximately $100 million gain and announced exploration of potential GreenSky sale.
macro_exposures: Provision for credit losses remained elevated at $615 million reflecting net charge-offs, credit card portfolio growth, and individual wholesale loan impairments. Commercial real estate market pressures intensified, contributing to $485 million in asset impairments within consolidated entities. The firm maintains exposure to persistent inflation, geopolitical tensions, and potential regulatory requirement increases impacting market-making activity and investment banking volumes.
forward_outlook: Management expects 2023 effective tax rate of approximately 22% and plans to increase stock repurchase levels in Q3 2023. The firm maintains through-the-cycle ROE targets of 14-16% and ROTE targets of 15-17%. Asset & Wealth Management targets mid-twenties pre-tax margins with alternative investments gross inflows target through 2024. Platform Solutions targets efficiency ratio below 100% for 2023 and pre-tax profitability by end-2025.
uncertainty_profile: The firm faces continued uncertainty from geopolitical risks, inflation concerns, and commercial real estate sector pressures. Proposed Basel III reforms could materially increase regulatory capital requirements, while FDIC proposed special assessments could cost approximately $400 million pre-tax. GreenSky's identifiable intangible assets remain subject to ongoing impairment evaluation, and credit loss provisions could increase under adverse economic scenarios.
